elements.name: Title
texts: "That's the Red Lake unless I mistake"

elements.name: Description
texts: "Now it seems there's a place they call Purgat're, so I must write it, my verse not admitting the O. But as for the venue I vow I'm perplext To say it's in this world or if in the next, Or whether in both for 'tis very well known That St Patrick at least has got one of his own In a tight little Island that stands in Lake Call'd Lough Derg : that's the Red Lake unless I mistake."

elements.name: Source
texts: Thomas Ingoldsby, The Ingoldsby Legends, The Lay of the Old Woman Clothed in Grey, Leslie, Shane, Saint Patrick's Purgatory: A Record from History and Literature, p. xxxii

elements.name: Publisher
texts: Burns Oats and Washbourne Ltd, London

elements.name: Rights
texts: Citation for the purposes of criticism

texts: "Now it seems there's a place they call Purgat're, so I must write it, my verse not admitting the O. But as for the venue I vow I'm perplext To say it's in this world or if in the next, Or whether in both for 'tis very well known That St Patrick at least has got one of his own In a tight little Island that stands in  Lake Call'd Lough Derg : that's the Red Lake unless I mistake."
